🔺 To demonstrate Iran’s maritime deterrence, these submarines surfaced during an operation dedicated to the martyrs of the Dena destroyer in the Strait of Hormuz. After carrying out formation maneuvers, they submerged again to continue their missions.

🔺 These light submarines can “bottom rest” on the seabed for long periods in the strategic waters of the Strait of Hormuz, allowing them to monitor and, if necessary, engage hostile vessels. This is part of their capability to track and neutralize enemy ships.

Iran's Navy Commander, Rear Admiral Irani:

🔺 Iran’s domestically built light submarines—known as dolphins of the Persian Gulf—are being deployed and expanded in the Strait of Hormuz based on threats, capabilities, and operational needs, remaining on alert and ready for action. https://t.co/qn0i8RU4x9

At least 26 people were martyred in a single day by Israeli bombing in southern Lebanon, according to #AlMayadeen’s correspondent, amid continued violations of the ceasefire and the ongoing occupation of  Lebanese territory.

#Lebanon’s Health Ministry also announced that one person was martyred and 13 others wounded, including six children and two women, in an Israeli attack targeting the town of  Bedias in the Tyre district.

In response to the escalating aggression, Hezbollah has carried out numerous operations against  Israeli occupation forces using FPV drones, one-way attack drones, and rocket fire, maintaining pressure on occupation positions along the border.

Israeli media, citing the occupation army, acknowledged that explosive-laden FPV drones have become “the most dangerous threat”  facing Israeli forces in Lebanon. Channel 12 further reported that  Hezbollah has succeeded in keeping the Israeli military in a constant state of defense and attrition.

Female Palestinian detainees in Israeli prisons are facing systematic abuses that constitute war crimes under international law, Hamas stated on Saturday, calling for their immediate release.

In a statement, the movement said that "the escalation of crimes and violations against Palestinian female detainees in Zionist occupation prisons" includes torture, isolation, deliberate medical neglect, and deprivation of basic human rights.

"This constitutes a war crime and a flagrant violation of all international charters and laws, foremost of which the Fourth Geneva Convention," the statement read.

According to the movement, 87 female detainees, including pregnant women, ill detainees, and minors, are being held in harsh conditions. The statement said the detention of 87 female detainees in harsh conditions, along with strip searches and administrative detention without charges, reveals the "fascist face" of the Israeli government and its use of prisons to repress and break the will of the Palestinian people.

The statement also addressed the detention of women over social media activity, stating that "the pursuit and detention of Palestinian women based on their opinions or posts on social media confirms the escalating policy of organized terror practiced by the war criminal Netanyahu's government against the sons of our people."

The group called on international bodies to take action. It urged "the United Nations, international rights and humanitarian institutions, and the International Committee of the Red Cross to act urgently to document these crimes, pressure for the immediate release of all female detainees, and hold the occupation's leaders accountable for their ongoing crimes against our people and our female detainees."

#Hamas #Palestine #Gaza #WestBank

Alongside the ongoing Israeli aggression on residential areas in South Lebanon despite a supposed "ceasefire", the Israeli entity targeted vehicles across main roads in Mount Lebanon today.

Al Mayadeen's correspondent reported on the Israeli aggression targeting multiple towns across multiple areas in the South, alongside eastern and Mount Lebanon. 

In Mount Lebanon, the Chouf district, an Israeli aggression on the Moltaqa Al-Nahrayn area resulted in the martyrdom of one citizen and the injury of two others. Footage circulating online also showed an Israeli attack on vehicles in the Saadiyat area in the same district. 

In the Tyre district, Israeli aggression targeted multiple towns, including Burj Rahhal, where, according to our correspondent, three people were martyred due to the aggression.

The Israeli entity also launched aggression on multiple towns across the Bint Jbeil and Nabatieh districts. Our correspondent reported that a young girl was martyred alongside her father this morning in Nabatieh due to Israeli aggression in the area. 

In the Saida district, Israeli aggression targeted the towns of Zrariyeh and al-Saksakiyeh. A massacre was committed in al-Sakasakiyeh, which, according to an initial toll, the Israeli strike killed seven people, including a young girl, and injured 15 others, including three children.

In eastern Lebanon, Israeli aggression targeted the town of Qelya in western Bekaa.

#Lebanon #LebanonUnderAttack #SouthLebanon

In an interview for Al Mayadeen, Professor Mohammad Marandi highlighted that the supposed one-page agreement Axios shared a few days ago was "complete nonsense", asserting that it was meant for market manipulation. 

"But the Iranians said that there is no such document and there is no such agreement," he emphasized. 

Professor Marandi also stressed that the Iranians are not going to give up their peaceful nuclear program, adding, "They're not going to give Trump a deal better than that of Obama."

He added that at the moment, Iran's priority is not the nuclear program, it is peace.

"And so the discussions on the nuclear program will be left for a later date. That's what the Iranians have told the Americans. And for the Iranians right now, the priority is to have a ceasefire in Lebanon and, of course, in Palestine, but also to end the siege," Professor Marandi said.

@s_m_marandi 

#AlMayadeen #IranWar #WestAsia #Iran #US

Bahrain authorities have launched a sweeping campaign of raids and arrests targeting a large number of religious scholars, marking another violent escalation in the kingdom’s long-running repression of dissent and religious figures.

Bahrain is witnessing a broad security crackdown, with authorities carrying out intensive raids and arrests against numerous Shiite clerics across the country.

Those arrested reportedly include Sheikh Jassim al-Khayyat, Sheikh Munir al-Ma'touq, Sheikh Raed al-Sitri, Sheikh Ali Humaidan, Sheikh Ayoub al-Bahrani, and Sheikh Issa al-Mu’min.

As part of the campaign, Bahraini authorities also detained religious scholars and seminary teachers, including Sheikh Mahmoud al-Aali, Sheikh Mohammed Sanqour, and Sheikh Ali al-Sadadi.

The arrests also extended to Sheikh Ali Rahma, Sheikh Hani Al-Banna, Sheikh Fadel al-Zaki, Sheikh Ali al-Matghawi, Sheikh Ghazi al-Sammak, Sheikh Mohammed Jawad al-Shihabi, Sheikh Ali Sanad, Sheikh Hussein al-Mahrous, Sayyed Sadiq al-Maliki, Sheikh Ali Naji, Sayyed Hussein al-Karani, and Sheikh Ali Hassan al-Saibai.

#Bahrain #WestAsia
